questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All aqueous solutions contain  \( H^{+} \) (aq) ions.

options.label: B
options.text: The pH of all acid solutions is greater than zero.

options.label: C
options.text: All acidic compounds contain hydrogen as their constituent elements.

options.label: D
options.text: A ‘corrosive’ hazard warning label must be displayed on all reagent bottles containing acid solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following processes would NOT give oxygen?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Element Q belongs to Group II of the Periodic Table. It combines with element R to give an ionic compound with chemical formula  \( Q_{3}R_{2} \) . Which group of the Periodic Table does R belong to?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following salts CANNOT be prepared from the reaction of a metal with a dilute acid?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: A gel containing NaCl(aq),  \( K_{3}Fe(CN)_{6}(aq) \)  and phenolphthalein is yellow in colour. An iron nail is put into the gel and corrodes after a period of time. Which of the following colours would NOT be observed in the gel after the iron nail corrodes?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The conversion of nitrogen gas to nitric acid involves the following steps :
\( \mathrm{N}_{2}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{Step}1}\mathrm{NH}_{3}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{Step}2}\mathrm{NO}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{Step}3}\mathrm{NO}_{2}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{Step}4}\mathrm{HNO}_{3} \)
In which step is nitrogen reduced?

(1) [IMG:0.jpg]      (2) [IMG:1.jpg]
(3) [IMG:2.jpg]      (4) [IMG:3.jpg]
Which hook would corrode first?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In an experiment,  \( 25.0 \, cm^{3} \)  of HCl(aq) is measured with apparatus X and is placed in apparatus Y. The HCl(aq) in Y is then titrated with a standard NaOH(aq). Which of the following combinations is correct?
X    |    Y

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In an experiment to prepare calcium sulphate, excess dilute sulphuric acid is added to  \( 10.0 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( 1.0 \, mol \ dm^{-3} \)  calcium nitrate solution. Which of the following is the theoretical mass of the calcium sulphate obtained?

(Relative atomic masses: O = 16.0, S = 32.1, Ca = 40.1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
The structure of a certain polymer is shown below :
[IMG:0.jpg]

Which of the following is the systematic name of the monomer of this polymer?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: In the species below, the underlined atom is the central atom, and all non-central atoms have octet electronic arrangement. In which of them does the central atom NOT have octet electronic arrangement?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reactions :
(1)  \( CO_{2}(g) \)  +  \( CaO(s) \)  →  \( CaCO_{3}(s) \)   |  \( \Delta H_{1} \)
(2)  \( NH_{3}(g) \)  +  \( HBr(g) \)  →  \( NH_{4}Br(s) \)   |   \( \Delta H_{2} \)
(3)  \( HF(aq) \)  +  \( NaOH(aq) \)  →  \( NaF(aq) \)  +  \( H_{2}O(l) \)   |   \( \Delta H_{3} \)
(4)  \( NaHCO_{3}(aq) \)  +  \( HCl(aq) \)  →  \( NaCl(aq) \)  +  \( CO_{2}(g) \)  +  \( H_{2}O(l) \)   |   \( \Delta H_{4} \)

Which of the following represents enthalpy change of neutralisation?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
An electrolysis experiment is conducted using the set-up shown below :
[IMG:1.jpg]
What are the expected colours around X and Y after the experiment has been conducted for some time?
X    |    Y

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following information concerning metals W, X, Y and Z :

(1) Heating oxide of W gives metal W.
(2) Heating metal X in steam gives a colourless gas.
(3) Putting metal Y in  \( CH_{3}CO_{2}H(aq) \)  gives a colourless gas.
(4) Putting metal Z in  \( CuSO_{4}(aq) \)  gives a reddish-brown solid.

Which of these metals has the lowest reactivity?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning 'atom' is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All atoms do not carry net charges.

options.label: B
options.text: Mass is evenly distributed within an atom.

options.label: C
options.text: All atoms consist of protons, neutrons and electrons.

options.label: D
options.text: For all elements, atoms of the same element have the same mass number.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Consider the following set-up at the start of an experiment :
[IMG:0.jpg]

After a period of time, the concentration of  \( K_{2}Cr_{2}O_{7}(aq) \)  drops to 0.47 M. What is the concentration of  \( FeSO_{4}(aq) \)  at that time?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
An aqueous solution of potassium iodide turns yellow with time due to the following reaction :

\( 4KI(aq)+2CO_{2}(g)+O_{2}(g)\rightarrow2K_{2}CO_{3}(aq)+2I_{2}(aq) \)

(1) KI(aq) is oxidised by  \( O_{2}(g) \) .
(2) KI(aq) is oxidised by  \( CO_{2}(g) \) .
(3) The yellow colour is due to the  \( K_{2}CO_{3}(aq) \)  formed.

Which of the following statements concerning the above reaction is / are correct?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following combinations is / are correct?

Chemical reaction    |    Enthalpy change of reaction
(1)  \( 2H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow 2H_{2}(g) + O_{2}(g) \)    |    positive
(2)  \( 2CO(g) + O_{2}(g) \rightarrow 2CO_{2}(g) \)    |    positive
(3)  \( 2Na(s) + 2H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow 2NaOH(aq) + H_{2}(g) \)    |    negative

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following pairs of substances can be distinguished by using acidified  \( KMnO_{4}(aq) \) ?

(1) pent-1-ene and pent-2-ene
(2) cyclohexane and cyclohexene
(3) polyethene and poly(chloroethene)

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The set-up of an experiment is shown below :
[IMG:0.jpg]

Which of the following observations would be expected?

(1) Limewater turns milky.
(2)  \( Br_{2}(aq) \)  changes from brown to colourless.
(3) The flame is brick red in colour.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following observations would be expected when some calcium granules are put in cold water inside a test tube?

(1) A cloudy mixture is formed.
(2) The test tube becomes warm.
(3) Colourless gas bubbles are formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following are renewable energy sources?

(1) nuclear energy
(2) tidal energy
(3) biomass

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following can distinguish a sample of limestone powder from a sample of table salt?

(1) adding water
(2) performing a flame test
(3) adding dilute hydrochloric acid

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement: The boiling point of  \( H_{2}O \)  is lower than that of HF.
2nd statement: The electronegativity of oxygen is lower than that of fluorine.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning the Periodic Table is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The melting point of the Group I elements increases down the group.

options.label: B
options.text: The boiling point of the Group VII elements increases down the group.

options.label: C
options.text: The elements are arranged in the order of increasing relative atomic mass.

options.label: D
options.text: The electrical conductivity of the third period elements increases from left to right.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: How many geometrical isomers does  \( H_{3}C-CH=CH-CH=CH-CH_{3} \)  have?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the following reaction at a certain temperature :

\( 2\mathbf{X}\mathbf{Y}_{2}(\mathbf{g})\rightleftharpoons\mathbf{X}_{2}(\mathbf{g})+2\mathbf{Y}_{2}(\mathbf{g}) \)        \( K_{\mathrm{c}}=0.60\ mol\ dm^{-3} \)

An equilibrium mixture was obtained at this temperature as shown below :
[IMG:0.jpg]

What is n ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following pairs of chemicals, upon mixing under the same temperature, has the highest rate of gas formation?

options.label: A
options.text: 0.10 g of Zn powder and 100 cm\( ^{3} \) of 1.0 M HCl(aq)

options.label: B
options.text: 0.10 g of Zn granules and 200 cm\( ^{3} \) of 1.0 M HCl(aq)

options.label: C
options.text: 0.10 g of Zn granules and 200 cm\( ^{3} \) of 1.0 M H\( _{2} \)SO\( _{4} \)(aq)

options.label: D
options.text: 0.10 g of Zn powder and 100 cm\( ^{3} \) of 1.0 M H\( _{2} \)PO\( _{4} \)(aq)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of the antibiotic ‘amoxicillin’ is shown below :
[IMG:1.jpg]

Which of the following functional groups is / are present in amoxicillin?

(1) ester
(2) amide
(3) hydroxyl

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In a closed container and at a certain temperature, the following equilibrium was attained :

\( \mathrm{COCl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g}) + \mathrm{Cl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \)

Which of the following statements is / are correct?

(1) CO(g) and Cl₂(g) must be of the same concentration.
(2) The rate of decomposition of  \( \mathrm{COCl}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}) \)  is equal to the rate of formation of  \( \mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g}) \) .
(3) The equilibrium constant  \( K_{c} \)  for the reaction increases when the volume of the container increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
(1) [IMG:0.jpg]     (2) [IMG:1.jpg]     (3) [IMG:2.jpg]

Which of the following compounds can react with acidified potassium dichromate solution to form a ketone?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
(This question was cancelled)
Consider the following equilibrium reaction system in a closed container of fixed volume :

\( \mathrm{CO(g) + H_{2}O(g) \rightleftharpoons CO_{2}(g) + H_{2}(g)} \)   \( \Delta H < 0 \)

Which of the following, when applied to the system, would lead to an increase in the rate of formation of  \( H_{2}(g) \) ?

(1) adding CO(g)
(2) increasing the temperature
(3) adding a suitable catalyst

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
A polymer has the structure shown below :
[IMG:3.jpg]

Which of the following statements concerning the polymer is correct?

(1) Its intermolecular attraction is predominantly hydrogen bond.
(2) The polymer chains can be broken in the presence of dilute hydrochloric acid.
(3) The polymer chains can be broken in the presence of dilute sodium hydroxide solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement: The melting point of silicon is higher than that of aluminium.
2nd statement: The number of electrons in a silicon atom is greater than that in an aluminium atom.

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
1st statement: At room conditions, the volume of 1 mol of  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  is larger than that of 1 mol of  \( N_{2}(g) \) .
2nd statement: The number of atoms constituting 1 mol of  \( SO_{2}(g) \)  is greater than that constituting 1 mol of  \( N_{2}(g) \) .

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.
